incorrects.description: Student selected 5: confused items per group with number of groups

params.description: The 4 bags themselves highlight, not the items inside.

steps.text: You're counting what's inside each group. The question asks how many groups. Count the bags themselves.

incorrects.description: Student selected 8: may have added 4 groups + 5 items, or misunderstood the question

steps.text: Look at the bags. We're asking how many bags you see, not how much is inside them. Count the containers.

incorrects.description: Student selected 20: counted total items instead of groups

params.description: The 4 bags themselves highlight, distinguishing containers from contents.

steps.text: You counted all the items. The question asks how many groups. A group is one bag. How many bags are there?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: The 4 bags highlight one at a time in sequence: 1, 2, 3, 4.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags: 1 bag, 2 bags, 3 bags, 4 bags. There are 4 groups. Each group is a bag. The answer is 4 groups.
